Package com.apexfintechsolutions.ascendsdk.models.components

Class AlternativeInvestment

java.lang.Object
com.apexfintechsolutions.ascendsdk.models.components.AlternativeInvestment
public class AlternativeInvestment extends Object
AlternativeInvestment

An alternative investment is a financial asset that does not fall into one of the conventional investment categories.

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested Classes
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description

    The maximum number of shares allowed to purchase in a single order for this asset.

    The system-generated resource name of the alternative investment asset.

    Indicates whether non-retirement accounts (ex: individual) are eligible to hold the alternative investment asset.

    The fee amount charged per share of the Alternative Investment in USD.

    The ID of the Pre IPO Company associated with the Alternative Investment.

    recordState()
    Indicates the current state of the asset record.
    Optional<Boolean>
    redeemable()
    Indicates whether the asset is allowed to be redeemed.

    redemptionFrequency()
    Indicates how frequently an account can redeem the asset.

    reinvestmentDistributionAllowed()
    Indicates whether the asset allows reinvestment distributions.

    Indicates whether retirement accounts (ex: IRA) are eligible to hold the asset.

    The asset’s state in the Apex alternative investment platform.

    The asset’s strategy as specified by the issuer.

    subscriptionFrequency()
    Indicates how frequently the asset will accept subscriptions (that is, how often an account is allowed to buy the asset).

    subscriptionOrderProcess()
    The process used for subscriptions orders of this asset.

    subsequentSubscriptionAllowed()
    Indicates whether the asset allows subsequent purchase orders for existing investors.

    The asset’s tax reporting structure.

  • Constructor Details

    • AlternativeInvestment

      public AlternativeInvestment(Optional<String> assetId, Optional<Boolean> cashDistributionAllowed, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends CurrentPrice> currentPrice, Optional<String> cusip, Optional<String> description, Optional<Boolean> dividendEnabled, Optional<Boolean> initialSubscriptionAllowed, Optional<String> isin, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends Issuer> issuer, Optional<String> issuingRegionCode, Optional<Integer> maximumSubscriptionQuantity, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends MinimumInitialSubscriptionAmount> minimumInitialSubscriptionAmount, Optional<Integer> minimumSubscriptionQuantity, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends MinimumSubsequentSubscriptionAmount> minimumSubsequentSubscriptionAmount, Optional<String> name, Optional<Boolean> nonRetirementEligible, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends PerShareFeeAmount> perShareFeeAmount, Optional<String> preIpoCompanyId, Optional<? extends RecordState> recordState, Optional<Boolean> redeemable, Optional<? extends RedemptionFrequency> redemptionFrequency, Optional<Boolean> reinvestmentDistributionAllowed, Optional<Boolean> retirementEligible, Optional<String> shareClass, Optional<? extends AlternativeInvestmentState> state, Optional<? extends Strategy> strategy, Optional<? extends SubscriptionFrequency> subscriptionFrequency, Optional<? extends SubscriptionOrderProcess> subscriptionOrderProcess, Optional<Boolean> subsequentSubscriptionAllowed, Optional<? extends TaxReportingStructure> taxReportingStructure, Optional<String> title, Optional<? extends AlternativeInvestmentType> type)

    • AlternativeInvestment

      public AlternativeInvestment()

  • Method Details

    • assetId

      public Optional<String> assetId()
      The ID of the alternative asset specified in the request.

    • cashDistributionAllowed

      public Optional<Boolean> cashDistributionAllowed()
      Indicates whether the asset allows cash distributions. If `TRUE` the alternative order request can specify `CASH` as the `distribution type`.

    • currentPrice

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<CurrentPrice> currentPrice()
      The current price of one unit of the asset in USD.

    • cusip

      public Optional<String> cusip()
      The CUSIP of the asset. It is a nine-character alphanumeric identifier used to uniquely identify securities, particularly in the United States and Canada.

      A true CUSIP consists of: - A six-character identifier for the issuer (company, municipality, etc.). - A two-character identifier for the type of security (equity, debt, etc.) and the specific issue within the issuer. - A single check digit.

      Many alternative investments assets do not have a true CUSIP. In such cases, the system generates a synthetic CUSIP when the asset is created.

    • description

      public Optional<String> description()
      A plain-text description of the asset, specified by the issuer.

    • dividendEnabled

      public Optional<Boolean> dividendEnabled()
      Indicates whether the asset distributes a dividend.

    • initialSubscriptionAllowed

      public Optional<Boolean> initialSubscriptionAllowed()
      Indicates whether the asset allows initial subscriptions to new investors.

    • isin

      public Optional<String> isin()
      The ISIN of the asset. It is a twelve-character alphanumeric code used to uniquely identify a security globally, as well as facilitate clearing, reporting, and settlement of trades defined by [ISO 6166](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/International_Securities_Identification_Number).

      An ISIN consists of: - A two-letter country code. - A nine-character alphanumeric security identifier (National Securities Identifying Number or NSIN). - A single check digit.

      Not all alternative investment assets have an ISIN.

    • issuer

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<Issuer> issuer()
      The issuer entity of the alternative investment asset.

    • issuingRegionCode

      public Optional<String> issuingRegionCode()
      The Unicode CLDR region code of the issuing region (ex: US).

    • maximumSubscriptionQuantity

      public Optional<Integer> maximumSubscriptionQuantity()
      The maximum number of shares allowed to purchase in a single order for this asset.

    • minimumInitialSubscriptionAmount

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<MinimumInitialSubscriptionAmount> minimumInitialSubscriptionAmount()
      The minimum purchase amount required for an initial order of this asset. Value is in USD.

    • minimumSubscriptionQuantity

      public Optional<Integer> minimumSubscriptionQuantity()
      The minimum number of shares allowed to purchase in a single order for this asset.

    • minimumSubsequentSubscriptionAmount

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<MinimumSubsequentSubscriptionAmount> minimumSubsequentSubscriptionAmount()
      The minimum purchase amount required for subsequent orders of this asset. Value is in USD.

    • name

      public Optional<String> name()
      The system-generated resource name of the alternative investment asset. Format: assets/{asset}/alternativeInvestment Note that `{asset}` will be the `asset_id` value.

    • nonRetirementEligible

      public Optional<Boolean> nonRetirementEligible()
      Indicates whether non-retirement accounts (ex: individual) are eligible to hold the alternative investment asset. - If `TRUE` then non-retirement accounts are eligible to hold the asset. - If `FALSE` then non-retirement accounts are not eligible to hold the asset.

    • perShareFeeAmount

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<PerShareFeeAmount> perShareFeeAmount()
      The fee amount charged per share of the Alternative Investment in USD. This is included in current price.

    • preIpoCompanyId

      public Optional<String> preIpoCompanyId()
      The ID of the Pre IPO Company associated with the Alternative Investment. Not all Alternative Investments are associated with a Pre IPO Company.

    • recordState

      public Optional<RecordState> recordState()
      Indicates the current state of the asset record. The record state identifies which record types the asset allows.

    • redeemable

      public Optional<Boolean> redeemable()
      Indicates whether the asset is allowed to be redeemed.

    • redemptionFrequency

      public Optional<RedemptionFrequency> redemptionFrequency()
      Indicates how frequently an account can redeem the asset.

    • reinvestmentDistributionAllowed

      public Optional<Boolean> reinvestmentDistributionAllowed()
      Indicates whether the asset allows reinvestment distributions.

      If `TRUE` the alternative order request can specify `REINVESTMENT` as the `distribution type`.

    • retirementEligible

      public Optional<Boolean> retirementEligible()
      Indicates whether retirement accounts (ex: IRA) are eligible to hold the asset. - If `TRUE` then retirement accounts are eligible to hold the asset. - If `FALSE` then retirement accounts are not eligible to hold the asset.

    • shareClass

      public Optional<String> shareClass()
      The asset’s designated share class.

    • state

      public Optional<AlternativeInvestmentState> state()
      The asset’s state in the Apex alternative investment platform.

    • strategy

      public Optional<Strategy> strategy()
      The asset’s strategy as specified by the issuer.

    • subscriptionFrequency

      public Optional<SubscriptionFrequency> subscriptionFrequency()
      Indicates how frequently the asset will accept subscriptions (that is, how often an account is allowed to buy the asset).

    • subscriptionOrderProcess

      public Optional<SubscriptionOrderProcess> subscriptionOrderProcess()
      The process used for subscriptions orders of this asset.

    • subsequentSubscriptionAllowed

      public Optional<Boolean> subsequentSubscriptionAllowed()
      Indicates whether the asset allows subsequent purchase orders for existing investors.

    • taxReportingStructure

      public Optional<TaxReportingStructure> taxReportingStructure()
      The asset’s tax reporting structure.

    • title

      public Optional<String> title()
      The name of the asset, in plain text, as specified by the issuer.

    • type

      public Optional<AlternativeInvestmentType> type()
      The type of alternative investment asset.

    • builder

      public static AlternativeInvestment.Builder builder()

    • withAssetId

      public AlternativeInvestment withAssetId(String assetId)
      The ID of the alternative asset specified in the request.

    • withAssetId

      public AlternativeInvestment withAssetId(Optional<String> assetId)
      The ID of the alternative asset specified in the request.

    • withCashDistributionAllowed

      public AlternativeInvestment withCashDistributionAllowed(boolean cashDistributionAllowed)
      Indicates whether the asset allows cash distributions. If `TRUE` the alternative order request can specify `CASH` as the `distribution type`.

    • withCashDistributionAllowed

      public AlternativeInvestment withCashDistributionAllowed(Optional<Boolean> cashDistributionAllowed)
      Indicates whether the asset allows cash distributions. If `TRUE` the alternative order request can specify `CASH` as the `distribution type`.

    • withCurrentPrice

      public AlternativeInvestment withCurrentPrice(CurrentPrice currentPrice)
      The current price of one unit of the asset in USD.

    • withCurrentPrice

      public AlternativeInvestment withCurrentPrice(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends CurrentPrice> currentPrice)
      The current price of one unit of the asset in USD.

    • withCusip

      public AlternativeInvestment withCusip(String cusip)
      The CUSIP of the asset. It is a nine-character alphanumeric identifier used to uniquely identify securities, particularly in the United States and Canada.

      A true CUSIP consists of: - A six-character identifier for the issuer (company, municipality, etc.). - A two-character identifier for the type of security (equity, debt, etc.) and the specific issue within the issuer. - A single check digit.

      Many alternative investments assets do not have a true CUSIP. In such cases, the system generates a synthetic CUSIP when the asset is created.

    • withCusip

      public AlternativeInvestment withCusip(Optional<String> cusip)
      The CUSIP of the asset. It is a nine-character alphanumeric identifier used to uniquely identify securities, particularly in the United States and Canada.

      A true CUSIP consists of: - A six-character identifier for the issuer (company, municipality, etc.). - A two-character identifier for the type of security (equity, debt, etc.) and the specific issue within the issuer. - A single check digit.

      Many alternative investments assets do not have a true CUSIP. In such cases, the system generates a synthetic CUSIP when the asset is created.

    • withDescription

      public AlternativeInvestment withDescription(String description)
      A plain-text description of the asset, specified by the issuer.

    • withDescription

      public AlternativeInvestment withDescription(Optional<String> description)
      A plain-text description of the asset, specified by the issuer.

      Indicates whether the asset distributes a dividend.

      Indicates whether the asset distributes a dividend.

    • withInitialSubscriptionAllowed

      public AlternativeInvestment withInitialSubscriptionAllowed(boolean initialSubscriptionAllowed)
      Indicates whether the asset allows initial subscriptions to new investors.

      Indicates whether the asset allows initial subscriptions to new investors.

    • withIsin

      public AlternativeInvestment withIsin(String isin)
      The ISIN of the asset. It is a twelve-character alphanumeric code used to uniquely identify a security globally, as well as facilitate clearing, reporting, and settlement of trades defined by [ISO 6166](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/International_Securities_Identification_Number).

      An ISIN consists of: - A two-letter country code. - A nine-character alphanumeric security identifier (National Securities Identifying Number or NSIN). - A single check digit.

      Not all alternative investment assets have an ISIN.

    • withIsin

      public AlternativeInvestment withIsin(Optional<String> isin)
      The ISIN of the asset. It is a twelve-character alphanumeric code used to uniquely identify a security globally, as well as facilitate clearing, reporting, and settlement of trades defined by [ISO 6166](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/International_Securities_Identification_Number).

      An ISIN consists of: - A two-letter country code. - A nine-character alphanumeric security identifier (National Securities Identifying Number or NSIN). - A single check digit.

      Not all alternative investment assets have an ISIN.

      The issuer entity of the alternative investment asset.

      The maximum number of shares allowed to purchase in a single order for this asset.

      public AlternativeInvestment withMaximumSubscriptionQuantity(Optional<Integer> maximumSubscriptionQuantity)
      The maximum number of shares allowed to purchase in a single order for this asset.

    • withMinimumInitialSubscriptionAmount

      public AlternativeInvestment withMinimumInitialSubscriptionAmount(MinimumInitialSubscriptionAmount minimumInitialSubscriptionAmount)
      The minimum purchase amount required for an initial order of this asset. Value is in USD.

    • withMinimumInitialSubscriptionAmount

      public AlternativeInvestment withMinimumInitialSubscriptionAmount(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends MinimumInitialSubscriptionAmount> minimumInitialSubscriptionAmount)
      The minimum purchase amount required for an initial order of this asset. Value is in USD.

    • withMinimumSubscriptionQuantity

      public AlternativeInvestment withMinimumSubscriptionQuantity(int minimumSubscriptionQuantity)
      The minimum number of shares allowed to purchase in a single order for this asset.

    • withMinimumSubscriptionQuantity

      public AlternativeInvestment withMinimumSubscriptionQuantity(Optional<Integer> minimumSubscriptionQuantity)
      The minimum number of shares allowed to purchase in a single order for this asset.

    • withMinimumSubsequentSubscriptionAmount

      public AlternativeInvestment withMinimumSubsequentSubscriptionAmount(MinimumSubsequentSubscriptionAmount minimumSubsequentSubscriptionAmount)
      The minimum purchase amount required for subsequent orders of this asset. Value is in USD.

    • withMinimumSubsequentSubscriptionAmount

      public AlternativeInvestment withMinimumSubsequentSubscriptionAmount(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends MinimumSubsequentSubscriptionAmount> minimumSubsequentSubscriptionAmount)
      The minimum purchase amount required for subsequent orders of this asset. Value is in USD.

    • withName

      public AlternativeInvestment withName(String name)
      The system-generated resource name of the alternative investment asset. Format: assets/{asset}/alternativeInvestment Note that `{asset}` will be the `asset_id` value.

    • withName

      public AlternativeInvestment withName(Optional<String> name)
      The system-generated resource name of the alternative investment asset. Format: assets/{asset}/alternativeInvestment Note that `{asset}` will be the `asset_id` value.

    • withNonRetirementEligible

      public AlternativeInvestment withNonRetirementEligible(boolean nonRetirementEligible)
      Indicates whether non-retirement accounts (ex: individual) are eligible to hold the alternative investment asset. - If `TRUE` then non-retirement accounts are eligible to hold the asset. - If `FALSE` then non-retirement accounts are not eligible to hold the asset.

    • withNonRetirementEligible

      public AlternativeInvestment withNonRetirementEligible(Optional<Boolean> nonRetirementEligible)
      Indicates whether non-retirement accounts (ex: individual) are eligible to hold the alternative investment asset. - If `TRUE` then non-retirement accounts are eligible to hold the asset. - If `FALSE` then non-retirement accounts are not eligible to hold the asset.

    • withPerShareFeeAmount

      public AlternativeInvestment withPerShareFeeAmount(PerShareFeeAmount perShareFeeAmount)
      The fee amount charged per share of the Alternative Investment in USD. This is included in current price.

    • withPerShareFeeAmount

      public AlternativeInvestment withPerShareFeeAmount(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends PerShareFeeAmount> perShareFeeAmount)
      The fee amount charged per share of the Alternative Investment in USD. This is included in current price.

    • withPreIpoCompanyId

      public AlternativeInvestment withPreIpoCompanyId(String preIpoCompanyId)
      The ID of the Pre IPO Company associated with the Alternative Investment. Not all Alternative Investments are associated with a Pre IPO Company.

    • withPreIpoCompanyId

      public AlternativeInvestment withPreIpoCompanyId(Optional<String> preIpoCompanyId)
      The ID of the Pre IPO Company associated with the Alternative Investment. Not all Alternative Investments are associated with a Pre IPO Company.

    • withRecordState

      public AlternativeInvestment withRecordState(RecordState recordState)
      Indicates the current state of the asset record. The record state identifies which record types the asset allows.

    • withRecordState

      public AlternativeInvestment withRecordState(Optional<? extends RecordState> recordState)
      Indicates the current state of the asset record. The record state identifies which record types the asset allows.

    • withRedeemable

      public AlternativeInvestment withRedeemable(boolean redeemable)
      Indicates whether the asset is allowed to be redeemed.

    • withRedeemable

      public AlternativeInvestment withRedeemable(Optional<Boolean> redeemable)
      Indicates whether the asset is allowed to be redeemed.

    • withRedemptionFrequency

      public AlternativeInvestment withRedemptionFrequency(RedemptionFrequency redemptionFrequency)
      Indicates how frequently an account can redeem the asset.

    • withRedemptionFrequency

      public AlternativeInvestment withRedemptionFrequency(Optional<? extends RedemptionFrequency> redemptionFrequency)
      Indicates how frequently an account can redeem the asset.

    • withReinvestmentDistributionAllowed

      public AlternativeInvestment withReinvestmentDistributionAllowed(boolean reinvestmentDistributionAllowed)
      Indicates whether the asset allows reinvestment distributions.

      If `TRUE` the alternative order request can specify `REINVESTMENT` as the `distribution type`.

    • withReinvestmentDistributionAllowed

      public AlternativeInvestment withReinvestmentDistributionAllowed(Optional<Boolean> reinvestmentDistributionAllowed)
      Indicates whether the asset allows reinvestment distributions.

      If `TRUE` the alternative order request can specify `REINVESTMENT` as the `distribution type`.

    • withRetirementEligible

      public AlternativeInvestment withRetirementEligible(boolean retirementEligible)
      Indicates whether retirement accounts (ex: IRA) are eligible to hold the asset. - If `TRUE` then retirement accounts are eligible to hold the asset. - If `FALSE` then retirement accounts are not eligible to hold the asset.

    • withRetirementEligible

      public AlternativeInvestment withRetirementEligible(Optional<Boolean> retirementEligible)
      Indicates whether retirement accounts (ex: IRA) are eligible to hold the asset. - If `TRUE` then retirement accounts are eligible to hold the asset. - If `FALSE` then retirement accounts are not eligible to hold the asset.

    • withShareClass

      public AlternativeInvestment withShareClass(String shareClass)
      The asset’s designated share class.

    • withShareClass

      public AlternativeInvestment withShareClass(Optional<String> shareClass)
      The asset’s designated share class.

    • withState

      public AlternativeInvestment withState(AlternativeInvestmentState state)
      The asset’s state in the Apex alternative investment platform.

    • withState

      public AlternativeInvestment withState(Optional<? extends AlternativeInvestmentState> state)
      The asset’s state in the Apex alternative investment platform.

    • withStrategy

      public AlternativeInvestment withStrategy(Strategy strategy)
      The asset’s strategy as specified by the issuer.

    • withStrategy

      public AlternativeInvestment withStrategy(Optional<? extends Strategy> strategy)
      The asset’s strategy as specified by the issuer.

    • withSubscriptionFrequency

      public AlternativeInvestment withSubscriptionFrequency(SubscriptionFrequency subscriptionFrequency)
      Indicates how frequently the asset will accept subscriptions (that is, how often an account is allowed to buy the asset).

    • withSubscriptionFrequency

      public AlternativeInvestment withSubscriptionFrequency(Optional<? extends SubscriptionFrequency> subscriptionFrequency)
      Indicates how frequently the asset will accept subscriptions (that is, how often an account is allowed to buy the asset).

    • withSubscriptionOrderProcess

      public AlternativeInvestment withSubscriptionOrderProcess(SubscriptionOrderProcess subscriptionOrderProcess)
      The process used for subscriptions orders of this asset.

    • withSubscriptionOrderProcess

      public AlternativeInvestment withSubscriptionOrderProcess(Optional<? extends SubscriptionOrderProcess> subscriptionOrderProcess)
      The process used for subscriptions orders of this asset.

    • withSubsequentSubscriptionAllowed

      public AlternativeInvestment withSubsequentSubscriptionAllowed(boolean subsequentSubscriptionAllowed)
      Indicates whether the asset allows subsequent purchase orders for existing investors.

    • withSubsequentSubscriptionAllowed

      public AlternativeInvestment withSubsequentSubscriptionAllowed(Optional<Boolean> subsequentSubscriptionAllowed)
      Indicates whether the asset allows subsequent purchase orders for existing investors.

    • withTaxReportingStructure

      public AlternativeInvestment withTaxReportingStructure(TaxReportingStructure taxReportingStructure)
      The asset’s tax reporting structure.

    • withTaxReportingStructure

      public AlternativeInvestment withTaxReportingStructure(Optional<? extends TaxReportingStructure> taxReportingStructure)
      The asset’s tax reporting structure.

    • withTitle

      public AlternativeInvestment withTitle(String title)
      The name of the asset, in plain text, as specified by the issuer.

    • withTitle

      public AlternativeInvestment withTitle(Optional<String> title)
      The name of the asset, in plain text, as specified by the issuer.

    • withType

      public AlternativeInvestment withType(AlternativeInvestmentType type)
      The type of alternative investment asset.

    • withType

      public AlternativeInvestment withType(Optional<? extends AlternativeInvestmentType> type)
      The type of alternative investment asset.
